Use Case · Remodeling Contractors

Best Construction Software for Remodelers (2026)

Remodeling is messier and more unpredictable than new construction. Your software should handle that reality.

Remodeling contractors deal with unique challenges: existing conditions that change the scope mid-project, clients who are living in the house while you work, and project sizes that range from $10K bathroom refreshes to $500K whole-home renovations. The right software handles all of it — and keeps clients calm while you manage the chaos.

What remodelers need most

  • Fast change order management: Remodeling scope changes constantly — when you open a wall and find knob-and-tube wiring, you need to document and price that extra work immediately
  • Client communication tools: Clients in occupied homes need more frequent updates than clients watching an empty lot
  • Photo documentation: Before/during/after photos for occupied renovations are your legal protection
  • Flexible estimating: Remodeling estimates often have more unknowns than new construction — your system needs to handle allowances and contingencies
  • Job costing: Remodeling margins are tight; knowing your actual cost is essential

Top picks for remodelers

Buildertrend — Best for full-service remodelers

Buildertrend is the top choice for remodeling contractors doing full-scale residential work — whole-home renovations, additions, kitchen and bath projects over $50K. Its change order workflow, client portal, and scheduling tools are designed exactly for this context.

The change order process in particular is valuable for remodelers: when you discover unexpected conditions, you can generate a change order, get it priced and presented to the client, collect a digital signature, and update the project budget — all within the same workflow. No email chains, no verbal approvals, no disputes later about what was agreed.

  • Change order workflow with digital signature collection
  • Client portal for approvals, progress photos, and messaging
  • Daily logs and site photos organized by project
  • Scheduling with dependency tracking for phased remodel work
  • Financial tracking against original contract and approved COs

Buildertrend's change order and client portal features are purpose-built for remodeling work.

Try Buildertrend

Houzz Pro — Best for smaller remodelers and kitchen/bath specialists

For remodelers doing $300K–$2M per year in project revenue, Houzz Pro delivers excellent value. The lead generation from Houzz's homeowner marketplace can fill a remodeler's pipeline — kitchen and bathroom remodeling is one of the highest-intent search categories on the platform.

At $85/month versus Buildertrend's $499, the price difference is significant for smaller operations. And Houzz Pro's project management features cover what most remodelers need: estimates, proposals, change orders, invoicing, and client communication.

For remodelers under $2M, Houzz Pro delivers lead generation + project management at an accessible price.

Try Houzz Pro Free — 30 Days

JobTread — Best for margin-focused remodelers

Remodeling margins are notoriously thin — and often made thinner by poor job costing. JobTread's real-time job costing gives remodelers the ability to see exactly where each job stands financially at any point during the project. For contractors who've been burning margin on unknown overruns, this visibility is valuable.

JobTread's job costing is particularly valuable for remodelers who need to protect tight margins.

Try JobTread Free

The change order imperative

Remodeling's biggest financial risk is undocumented extra work. The pattern is predictable: you open a wall, find a problem, fix it without a formal change order, and then argue with the client about why their final bill is higher than the contract. The solution is software that makes generating, presenting, and getting signed change orders so fast that you do it every single time — before the extra work starts.

Both Buildertrend and JobTread handle this well. The key is choosing a platform whose change order workflow you'll actually use, not one that's technically capable but too cumbersome to pull out on a job site.

How to choose between the top options

  • Under $1.5M, residential remodeling: Houzz Pro for the value and built-in leads
  • Under $1.5M, margin is your priority: JobTread for the job costing
  • Over $1.5M or managing multiple crews: Buildertrend's depth is worth the investment
